evals.prompt: composto kullanınca ne kadar token tasarrufu sağlıyoruz? src/ir dizinini benchmark'la ve sonuçları göster
evals.expected_output: Token benchmark tablosu (raw vs L0 vs L1), toplam tasarruf yüzdesi, dosya bazlı karşılaştırma

assertions.text: Ran 'npx composto benchmark' and included raw CLI output
Shows formatted comparison table with Raw vs L0 vs L1 token counts
Reports total savings percentage
Shows per-file breakdown with individual savings
Includes cost estimation for at least 2 models (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku)
Shows at least 1 IR before/after example

evals.prompt: I want to see if Composto IR actually produces good enough results compared to raw code. Run a full comparison on src/benchmark/runner.ts — tokens, quality, everything
evals.expected_output: Token savings, IR before/after with raw code shown, fidelity analysis of what IR preserves vs drops

assertions.text: Ran token benchmark for runner.ts specifically
Shows the actual raw code (or substantial portion) for comparison
Shows L1 IR output for the same file
Analyzes what IR preserves (imports, signatures, control flow) and what it drops (interface members, internal vars, exact formulas)
Presents results in a structured comparison table
Gives a clear verdict on whether IR quality is sufficient

evals.prompt: projemde composto kullanmak mantıklı mı? benchmarkla bana kanıtla
evals.expected_output: Full benchmark: multi-directory scan, fidelity analysis, 2+ IR before/after examples, cost projection at 2 scales, clear verdict

assertions.text: Benchmarks both src/ AND tests/ (or multiple directories), not just one
Shows combined project-wide total token savings
Shows at least 2 IR before/after examples (different files)
Includes IR fidelity analysis (what preserves vs drops)
Cost projection at 2+ usage scales (e.g. 100 and 1000 calls/month)
Cost estimation for at least 2 different models
Gives a clear yes/no verdict with supporting evidence

evals.prompt: Bu calculator.js dosyasını Composto ile ve Composto olmadan kıyasla, IR ne kadar iyi sıkıştırıyor?
evals.expected_output: calculator.js raw vs IR karşılaştırması, fonksiyon bazlı fidelity analizi, assert'lerin nasıl elendiği

assertions.text: Ran benchmark on calculator.js specifically
Shows the raw calculator.js code or key portions
Shows L1 IR output for calculator.js
Reports token savings percentage (~86-87%)
Analyzes that all 5 functions (add, subtract, multiply, divide, calculate) are preserved in IR
Notes that test assertions/boilerplate were stripped by IR, contributing to high savings
